name: 5q13_q14_NUMT_major_mitochondrial_insertion
description: 5q13.3-q14.3 NUMT (nuclear-mitochondrial-DNA) insertion — the largest known continuous NUMT in the human genome. A ~620 kb nuclear segment carries a near-complete mitochondrial-genome insertion (~14 kb) at ~98% identity to the rCRS mtDNA. False-positive heteroplasmy and mtDNA-mapping pile-ups in short-read data originate predominantly from this and a smaller chr1p36 NUMT.

architecture.internal_structure: Single nuclear insertion of a near-complete mtDNA genome (~14.9 kb of the 16.5 kb rCRS) at ~98% identity; flanked by unique nuclear sequence with no further mtDNA-like tracts within 1 Mb. Common confounder for cell-free DNA mtDNA quantification.
